title: Applehv podman machine rm --force sometimes breaks
body:
### Issue Description

With a working or failed machine, `podman machine rm --force` always fails and exits non-zero.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman machine init ...options...`
2. `podman --log-level=debug machine rm --force`


### Describe the results you received

```
time="2023-10-27T17:43:21Z" level=info msg="./bin/darwin/podman filtering at log level debug"
time="2023-10-27T17:43:21Z" level=debug msg="Using Podman machine with `applehv` virtualization provider"
time="2023-10-27T17:43:21Z" level=debug msg="backoff wait time: 20ms"
time="2023-10-27T17:43:21Z" level=debug msg="backoff wait time: 40ms"
time="2023-10-27T17:43:21Z" level=debug msg="backoff wait time: 80ms"
time="2023-10-27T17:43:21Z" level=debug msg="backoff wait time: 160ms"
time="2023-10-27T17:43:21Z" level=debug msg="backoff wait time: 320ms"
time="2023-10-27T17:43:22Z" level=debug msg="backoff wait time: 640ms"
time="2023-10-27T17:43:22Z" level=debug msg="backoff wait time: 1.28s"
time="2023-10-27T17:43:24Z" level=debug msg="backoff wait time: 2.56s"
time="2023-10-27T17:43:26Z" level=debug msg="backoff wait time: 5.12s"
time="2023-10-27T17:43:31Z" level=debug msg="backoff wait time: 10.24s"
time="2023-10-27T17:43:41Z" level=debug msg="backoff wait time: 20.48s"
Error: failed waiting for vm to stop
```

### Describe the results you expected

```
time="2023-10-27T17:43:21Z" level=info msg="./bin/darwin/podman filtering at log level debug"
time="2023-10-27T17:43:21Z" level=debug msg="Using Podman machine with `applehv` virtualization provider"
...harmless debug messages...
```

(exit 0)

### podman info output

```yaml
OS: darwin/arm64
provider: applehv
version: 4.8.0-dev

Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:49543: connect: connection refused
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

```
Darwin ip-172-31-15-123.ec2.internal 22.6.0 Darwin Kernel Version 22.6.0: Wed Jul  5 22:22:52 PDT 2023; root:xnu-8796.141.3~6/RELEASE_ARM64_T8103 arm64 arm Darwin
```

### Additional information

IIRC, the `rm` command also seems to exit non-zero if there is no running vfkit process.  Since removal is the goal, it should always cleanup files and exit `0`.  Unless something truly horrible happens like lock-file removal permission denied.

comments.body:
```
Machine "podman-machine-default" started successfully
brentbaude ~/go/src/github.com/containers/podman [applehvrmforce] $ ./bin/darwin/podman --log-level debug machine rm -f
INFO[0000] ./bin/darwin/podman filtering at log level debug
DEBU[0000] Using Podman machine with `applehv` virtualization provider
DEBU[0000] backoff wait time: 20ms
DEBU[0000] connection refused: http://localhost:8081/vm/state
DEBU[0000] Called machine rm.PersistentPostRunE(./bin/darwin/podman --log-level debug machine rm -f)
DEBU[0000] Shutting down engines
brentbaude ~/go/src/github.com/containers/podman [applehvrmforce] $ ./bin/darwin/podman --log-level debug machine rm -f
INFO[0000] ./bin/darwin/podman filtering at log level debug
DEBU[0000] Using Podman machine with `applehv` virtualization provider
Error: "/Users/brentbaude/.config/containers/podman/machine/applehv/podman-machine-default.json": VM does not exist
DEBU[0000] Shutting down engines
```

Two thoughts:

1. try running the rm command twice, and check `$?` immediately after.
2. could running w/ or w/o admin access be "in-play" for this?

In the WIP Mac CI PR: Rm behavior is inconsistent, most of the time it fails, but I saw it work a few times the other day.  All w/o playing with changing `$HOME` and with `$TMPDIR == $HOME`.

I'm experimenting with redefining `$HOME`, and *not* defining `$TMPDIR`, will see if that changes symptoms at all.

Some new findings:  Overriding `$HOME` and `$TMPDIR` has no effect.  Running scripted podman machine commands work fine, rm still fails.  The e2e test behavior is also the same.

Edit: Ref. [run w/ overriden envars.](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6086633410265088)
I'm sorry, I haven't noticed.  I'm no longer running those commands in the new CI WIP.  I can try running them manually...
I'll close it for now, we can re-open it if we see it again, since last time I was in the CI VM I didn't see this.

...confirmed, problem is fixed:

```
MacM1-6:podman MacM1-6-worker$ bin/darwin/podman machine init --image-path "https://fedorapeople.org/groups/podman/testing/applehv/arm64/fedora-coreos-38.20230925.dev.0-applehv.aarch64.raw.gz"

Downloading VM image: fedora-coreos-38.20230925.dev.0-applehv.aarch64.raw.gz: done
Extracting compressed file: podman-machine-default_fedora-coreos-38.20230925.dev.0-applehv.aarch64.raw: done
Machine init complete
To start your machine run:

        podman machine start

MacM1-6:podman MacM1-6-worker$ bin/darwin/podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"

This machine is currently configured in rootless mode. If your containers
require root permissions (e.g. ports < 1024), or if you run into compatibility
issues with non-podman clients, you can switch using the following command:

        podman machine set --rootful

API forwarding listening on: /Users/MacM1-6-worker/ci/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/applehv/podman.sock

The system helper service is not installed; the default Docker API socket
address can't be used by podman. If you would like to install it, run the following commands:

        sudo /Users/MacM1-6-worker/ci/task-0123456789/podman/bin/darwin/podman-mac-helper install
        podman machine stop; podman machine start

                You can still connect Docker API clients by setting DOCKER_HOST using the
following command in your terminal session:

        export DOCKER_HOST='unix:///Users/MacM1-6-worker/ci/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/applehv/podman.sock'

Machine "podman-machine-default" started successfully
MacM1-6:podman MacM1-6-worker$ ./bin/darwin/podman --log-level=debug machine rm --force
INFO[0000] ./bin/darwin/podman filtering at log level debug
DEBU[0000] Using Podman machine with `applehv` virtualization provider
DEBU[0000] connection refused: http://localhost:8081/vm/state
INFO[0000] Going to stop gvproxy (PID 5751)
DEBU[0000] Called machine rm.PersistentPostRunE(./bin/darwin/podman --log-level=debug machine rm --force)
DEBU[0000] Shutting down engines
```

Just to be sure, I ran it a second time as well:

```
MacM1-6:podman MacM1-6-worker$ ./bin/darwin/podman --log-level=debug machine rm --force
INFO[0000] ./bin/darwin/podman filtering at log level debug
DEBU[0000] Using Podman machine with `applehv` virtualization provider
Error: podman-machine-default: VM does not exist
DEBU[0000] Shutting down engines
```

So we're good now.  Thanks!
